name: Medallions of Beef Forestiere
recipeIngredient: 6 slices beef tenderloin, each about 1/4 lb.
6 oz. fresh mushrooms, thinly sliced (oyster, shiitake or morels, if possible)
1/2 c. dry red wine, preferably zinfandel
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle the beef with salt and pepper.
2. Heat 1 tablespoon of butter in a saucepan and add shallots and garlic. Cook briefly, stirring, then add sliced mushrooms. Cook, stirring about 3 minutes.
3. Add half the dry red wine and stir. Cook until reduced almost by half. Add beef or veal broth. Bring to boil and add the remaining dry red wine and madeira wine (or vermouth). Cook until reduced by half. There should be about 1 1/4 cups of sauce left.
4. Heat the remaining tablespoon of butter in a heavy skillet and add the beef. Cook the meat on one side about 2-3 minutes, or until browned. Turn and cook the other side for another 2-3 minutes. Transfer the meat to a warm platter.
5. Add mushroom sauce to the skillet and stir to dissolve any brown particles that may cling to the edge of the skillet.
6. Swirl the remaining 4 tablespoons of butter into the sauce. Serve over the tenderloins.
